chunks.text: Hello everyone.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Welcome to this tutorial by SimplyLearn.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: In this video, you will learn about an interesting machine learning topic that is supervised versus
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: unsupervised versus reinforcement learning.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Let's discuss each of them in detail and understand when to use these algorithms along
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: with their applications.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: There are a number of algorithms used in the field of machine learning to solve complex
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: problems.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Each of these algorithms can be classified into a certain category.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So the different types of machine learning algorithms are supervised learning, unsupervised
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: learning and reinforcement learning.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now let's look at the definition of each of these learning techniques.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Supervised learning uses labeled data to train machine learning models.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Universal data means that the output is already known to you.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: The model just needs to map the inputs to the outputs.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: An example of supervised learning can be to train a machine that identifies the image
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: of an animal.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Below you can see, we have a trained model that identifies the picture of a cat.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Unsupervised learning uses unlabeled data to train machines.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Unlabeled data means there is no fixed output variable.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: The model learns from the data, discovers patterns and features in the data and returns
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: the output.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Here is an example of an unsupervised learning technique that uses the images of vehicles
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: to classify if it's a bus or a truck.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So the model learns by identifying the parts of a vehicle such as the length and width
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: of the vehicle, the front and rear end covers, roof heads, the types of wheels used, etc.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Based on these features, the model classifies if the vehicle is a bus or a truck.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Reinforcement learning trains a machine to take suitable accents and maximize reward in
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: a particular situation.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: It uses an agent and an environment to produce actions and rewards.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: The agent has a start and an end state, but there might be different parts for reaching
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: the end state like a maze.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: In this learning technique, there is no predefined target variable.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: An example of reinforcement learning is to train a machine that can identify the shape
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: of an object given a list of different objects such as square, triangle, rectangle or a circle.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: In the example shown, the model tries to predict the shape of the object which is a
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: square here.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now let's look at the different machine learning algorithms that come under these learning
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: techniques.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Some of the commonly used supervised learning algorithms are linear regression, logistic
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: regression, support vector machines, k nearest neighbors, decision tree, random forest and
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: knife base.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Examples of unsupervised learning algorithms are k-means clustering, hierarchical clustering,
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: db scan, principle component analysis and others.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Choosing the right algorithm depends on the type of problem you are trying to solve.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Some of the important reinforcement learning algorithms are Q learning, Monte Carlo, Sarsa
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: and DeepQ network.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now let's look at the approach in which these machine learning techniques work.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So supervised learning takes labeled inputs and maps it to known outputs, which means
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: you already know the target variable.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Nonsupervised learning finds patterns and understands the trends in the data to discover
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: the output.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So the model tries to label the data based on the features of the input data.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: While reinforcement learning follows trial and error method to get the desired solution.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: After accomplishing a task, the agent receives an award.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: An example could be to train a dog to catch the ball.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: If the dog learns to catch a ball, you give it a reward such as a biscuit.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now let's discuss the training process for each of these learning methods.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So supervised learning methods need external supervision to train machine learning models
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: and hence the name supervised.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: The need guidance and additional information to return the result.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Unsupervised learning techniques do not need any supervision to train models.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: They learn on their own and predict the output.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Similarly, reinforcement learning methods do not need any supervision to train machine
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: learning models.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: And with that, let's focus on the types of problems that can be solved using these three
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: types of machine learning techniques.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So supervised learning is generally used for classification and regression problems.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: We'll see the examples in the next slide.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: And unsupervised learning is used for clustering and association problems.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: While reinforcement learning is reward based, so for every task or for every step completed,
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: there will be a reward received by the agent.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: And if the task is not achieved correctly, there will be some penalty used.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now let's look at a few applications of supervised unsupervised and reinforcement learning.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: As we saw earlier, supervised learning are used to solve classification and regression
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: problems.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: For example, you can predict the weather for a particular day based on humidity, precipitation,
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: wind speed and pressure values.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: You can use supervised learning algorithms to forecast sales for the next month or the
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: next quarter for different products.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Similarly, you can use it for stock price analysis or identifying if a cancer cell is
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: malignant or benign.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now talking about the applications of unsupervised learning, we have customer segmentation.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So based on customer behavior, likes, dislikes and interests, you can segment and cluster
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: similar customers into a group.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Another example where unsupervised learning algorithms are used is customer churn analysis.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Now let's see what applications we have in reinforcement learning.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: So reinforcement learning algorithms are widely used in the gaming industries to build games.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: It is also used to train robots to perform human tasks.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: And with that, we have come to the end of this video on supervised versus unsupervised
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: versus reinforcement learning.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: I hope you liked this video.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: If you enjoyed watching this video, then please subscribe to the Simple Launch channel
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: and hit the bell icon to never miss an update.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

chunks.text: Thank you for watching and keep learning.
chunks.topic: Supervised vs Unsupervised vs Reinforcement Learning

text: Hello everyone. Welcome to this tutorial by SimplyLearn. In this video, you will learn about an interesting machine learning topic that is supervised versus unsupervised versus reinforcement learning. Let's discuss each of them in detail and understand when to use these algorithms along with their applications. There are a number of algorithms used in the field of machine learning to solve complex problems. Each of these algorithms can be classified into a certain category. So the different types of machine learning algorithms are supervised learning, unsupervised learning and reinforcement learning. Now let's look at the definition of each of these learning techniques. Supervised learning uses labeled data to train machine learning models. Universal data means that the output is already known to you. The model just needs to map the inputs to the outputs. An example of supervised learning can be to train a machine that identifies the image of an animal. Below you can see, we have a trained model that identifies the picture of a cat. Unsupervised learning uses unlabeled data to train machines. Unlabeled data means there is no fixed output variable. The model learns from the data, discovers patterns and features in the data and returns the output. Here is an example of an unsupervised learning technique that uses the images of vehicles to classify if it's a bus or a truck. So the model learns by identifying the parts of a vehicle such as the length and width of the vehicle, the front and rear end covers, roof heads, the types of wheels used, etc. Based on these features, the model classifies if the vehicle is a bus or a truck. Reinforcement learning trains a machine to take suitable accents and maximize reward in a particular situation. It uses an agent and an environment to produce actions and rewards. The agent has a start and an end state, but there might be different parts for reaching the end state like a maze. In this learning technique, there is no predefined target variable. An example of reinforcement learning is to train a machine that can identify the shape of an object given a list of different objects such as square, triangle, rectangle or a circle. In the example shown, the model tries to predict the shape of the object which is a square here. Now let's look at the different machine learning algorithms that come under these learning techniques. Some of the commonly used supervised learning algorithms are linear regression, logistic regression, support vector machines, k nearest neighbors, decision tree, random forest and knife base. Examples of unsupervised learning algorithms are k-means clustering, hierarchical clustering, db scan, principle component analysis and others. Choosing the right algorithm depends on the type of problem you are trying to solve. Some of the important reinforcement learning algorithms are Q learning, Monte Carlo, Sarsa and DeepQ network. Now let's look at the approach in which these machine learning techniques work. So supervised learning takes labeled inputs and maps it to known outputs, which means you already know the target variable. Nonsupervised learning finds patterns and understands the trends in the data to discover the output. So the model tries to label the data based on the features of the input data. While reinforcement learning follows trial and error method to get the desired solution. After accomplishing a task, the agent receives an award. An example could be to train a dog to catch the ball. If the dog learns to catch a ball, you give it a reward such as a biscuit. Now let's discuss the training process for each of these learning methods. So supervised learning methods need external supervision to train machine learning models and hence the name supervised. The need guidance and additional information to return the result. Unsupervised learning techniques do not need any supervision to train models. They learn on their own and predict the output. Similarly, reinforcement learning methods do not need any supervision to train machine learning models. And with that, let's focus on the types of problems that can be solved using these three types of machine learning techniques. So supervised learning is generally used for classification and regression problems. We'll see the examples in the next slide. And unsupervised learning is used for clustering and association problems. While reinforcement learning is reward based, so for every task or for every step completed, there will be a reward received by the agent. And if the task is not achieved correctly, there will be some penalty used. Now let's look at a few applications of supervised unsupervised and reinforcement learning. As we saw earlier, supervised learning are used to solve classification and regression problems. For example, you can predict the weather for a particular day based on humidity, precipitation, wind speed and pressure values. You can use supervised learning algorithms to forecast sales for the next month or the next quarter for different products. Similarly, you can use it for stock price analysis or identifying if a cancer cell is malignant or benign. Now talking about the applications of unsupervised learning, we have customer segmentation. So based on customer behavior, likes, dislikes and interests, you can segment and cluster similar customers into a group. Another example where unsupervised learning algorithms are used is customer churn analysis. Now let's see what applications we have in reinforcement learning. So reinforcement learning algorithms are widely used in the gaming industries to build games. It is also used to train robots to perform human tasks. And with that, we have come to the end of this video on supervised versus unsupervised versus reinforcement learning. I hope you liked this video. If you enjoyed watching this video, then please subscribe to the Simple Launch channel and hit the bell icon to never miss an update. Thank you for watching and keep learning.
